root_text.an4.67:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an4.67:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ aññataro bhikkhu ahinā daṭṭho kālaṅkato hoti.
root_text.an4.67:1.3: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an4.67:1.4: “idha, bhante, sāvatthiyaṁ aññataro bhikkhu ahinā daṭṭho kālaṅkato”ti.
root_text.an4.67:2.1: “Na hi nūna so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cattāri ahirājakulāni mettena cittena phari.
root_text.an4.67:2.2: Sace hi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cattāri ahirājakulāni mettena cittena phareyya, na hi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ahinā daṭṭho kālaṁ kareyya.
root_text.an4.67:3.2: Virūpakkhaṁ ahirājakulaṁ, erāpathaṁ ahirājakulaṁ, chabyāputtaṁ ahirājakulaṁ, kaṇhāgotamakaṁ ahirājakulaṁ.
root_text.an4.67:3.3: Na hi nūna so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu imāni cattāri ahirājakulāni mettena cittena phari.
root_text.an4.67:3.4: Sace hi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu imāni cattāri ahirājakulāni mettena cittena phareyya, na hi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ahinā daṭṭho kālaṁ kareyya.
root_text.an4.67:4.1: Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, imāni cattāri ahirājakulāni mettena cittena pharituṁ attaguttiyā attarakkhāya attaparittāyāti.
root_text.an4.67:10.3: Katā me rakkhā katā me parittā,

translation_text.an4.67:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an4.67:1.2: Now, at that time a monk in Sāvatthī passed away due to a snake bite.
translation_text.an4.67:1.3: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an4.67:1.4: “Sir, a monk in Sāvatthī has passed away due to a snake bite.”
translation_text.an4.67:2.1: “Mendicants, that monk mustn’t have spread a mind of love to the four royal snake families.
translation_text.an4.67:2.2: If he had, he wouldn’t have died due to a snake bite.
translation_text.an4.67:3.2: The royal snake families of Virūpakkha, Erāpatha, Chabyāputta, and Kaṇhāgotamaka. …
translation_text.an4.67:4.1: Mendicants, I urge you to spread a mind of love to the four royal snake families, for your own safety, security, and protection.
translation_text.an4.67:7.1: May the footless not harm me!
translation_text.an4.67:7.2: May I not be harmed by the two-footed!
translation_text.an4.67:7.3: May the four-footed not harm me!
translation_text.an4.67:7.4: May I not be harmed by the many-footed!
translation_text.an4.67:8.1: All sentient beings, all living things,
translation_text.an4.67:8.3: may they see only nice things,
translation_text.an4.67:8.4: may bad not come to anyone.
translation_text.an4.67:10.3: I’ve made this safeguard, I’ve made this protection:
translation_text.an4.67:10.5: And so I revere the Blessed One,
translation_text.an4.67:10.6: I revere the seven perfectly awakened Buddhas.”

variant_text.an4.67:2.1: Na hi nūna → nahanūna (bj, cck, sya1ed, km, pts1ed); naha nūna (sya2ed)
variant_text.an4.67:10.3: rakkhā katā me parittā → kataṁ me parittaṁ (?)
